mnemonics: INPUTS, OUTPUTS, SCRIPTS & SIGS - Every Transaction Is Our Secure System
keyPoints: A transaction is a data structure that encodes the transfer of value between participants
It consists of inputs (references to previous outputs) and outputs (instructions for sending value)
Outputs are locked with a scripting system and can only be spent by satisfying the lock conditions
commonMistakes: Thinking transactions directly move coins between wallets (they actually update the UTXO set)
Believing transaction size is fixed (it varies based on number of inputs/outputs)
Assuming all transaction data is stored forever on-chain (some data can be pruned)
